confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_aa5bc553-2ec4-46d6-9812-7d7a9b66f915 read back as archived

evidence: Task 62 review found an execution-boundary bug in EE-MCP path admission.
The review flow also exposed reviewer identity mismatch for narada-andrey.Kevin.
Kevin recorded review rejection and nudged Bob but did not submit CAPA until prompted by Operator.
This repeats a class of failures where agents handle the immediate task but fail to route systemic process defects into Narada learning machinery.
proposal: Define CAPA-grade trigger vocabulary for agents: authority-boundary bug, safety/secret boundary bug, lifecycle/roster authority mismatch, tooling workaround that changes reviewer/principal, repeated operator correction, or defect likely to recur across Sites.
Add role-loop checklist item: after any rejected review or blocking finding, decide CAPA needed yes/no with one-line rationale; if yes, submit before final response.
Add mechanical support where possible: task review rejected with blocking findings should produce a CAPA recommendation or require --capa-none-reason.
Add self-CAPA expectation: if Operator points out missing CAPA after an incident, submit both the original CAPA and a meta-CAPA for the missed escalation.
payload.recommendation: Treat as CAPA for CAPA detection/admission ergonomics and agent role-loop discipline.
payload.summary: After task 62 review, Kevin reported findings and nudged Bob but did not immediately submit a CAPA, despite observing a blocking authority-boundary defect and reviewer identity/roster mismatch. Operator had to identify the missing CAPA step. Narada needs a guardrail so CAPA-grade incidents automatically prompt or require CAPA submission.
payload.title: CAPA: Architect failed to submit CAPA after CAPA-grade review incident

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
